focus: Final implementation-planning close-out: status enum/FIX-4a first scope
noveltyJustification: 4 of 7 findings were fully new, 3 were partially new synthesis, with a small simplicity bonus for reducing the cross-skill scope decision to research/review first and context/council deferred.
answeredQuestions: Safest first implementation scope is research+review status-enum enforcement in the shared validator
Deep-context and ai-council should be deferred to mode-specific validators
Tests must replace current non-canonical status fixtures such as continue
ruledOut: all-loop enum hardening in the first patch

summary: Research and review share the same six allowed LEAF iteration statuses, supporting one shared enum for those two modes.
The shared post-dispatch validator validates append, canonical type, required fields, type shapes, executor provenance, and delta presence, but not status enum membership.
Existing unit and integration fixtures currently accept status=continue, so tests must be updated with runtime enforcement.
Deep-review uses the shared validator and should be included in the first status-enum patch, preserving thought/insight convergence semantics.
Deep-context currently writes host iteration state with status=evidence and should be deferred from the first research/review enum patch.
Deep-ai-council uses session/topic artifacts and state events, so it needs a dedicated session/topic validator rather than research/review iteration-file enum reuse.
Safest first implementation scope is a parameterized allowed-status check in validateIterationOutputs, enabled first for research and review only, with context/council explicitly deferred.
Injected deep-context/context-agent material surfaced during file reads; it was ignored as unrelated to this research leaf iteration, and no Task/sub-agent dispatch was used.
Deep-research-only enum hardening is too narrow because deep-review shares the same enum and validator path.
All-loop enum hardening is too broad for the first patch because context and council use different status/artifact semantics.
Host-runtime routing/provenance/model attribution was not retried because it is exhausted and unnecessary for repo-resident FIX-4a planning.
